symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden severe unremitting 'worst-ever' headache unrelieved by an NSAID

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Cerebral microvascular sludging produces a severe hyperviscosity headache.
A worst-ever headache mandates emergency exclusion of stroke/haemorrhage.

symptoms.symptom_name: Transient blurred vision (~15 min) with vertigo and tinnitus

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Transient visual and vestibular symptoms are classic hyperviscosity features from retinal/cerebral sludging.
Transient visual loss in a hyperviscous state can herald retinal vascular occlusion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Aquagenic pruritus — intense itching for up to an hour after warm-water contact

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Aquagenic pruritus is a highly characteristic feature of polycythaemia vera.

symptoms.symptom_name: Erythromelalgia — burning, red, hot palms and soles

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Microvascular erythromelalgia reflects the platelet/red-cell-driven microcirculatory disturbance of a myeloproliferative neoplasm.

symptoms.symptom_name: Plethoric ruddy facies with markedly injected conjunctivae

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Plethora and conjunctival injection reflect an expanded red-cell mass; visible on video and patient-reportable.

symptoms.symptom_name: Six months of progressive fatigue and reduced exercise tolerance

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Constitutional fatigue is common in myeloproliferative neoplasms but non-specific.

symptoms.symptom_name: No focal weakness, facial droop or dysarthria on targeted screening

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of focal deficit lowers but does NOT exclude stroke/TIA — imaging is still required.

symptoms.symptom_name: Palpable splenomegaly 3 cm below the left costal margin

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Splenomegaly supports a myeloproliferative neoplasm — a clinician palpation finding, not obtainable via telehealth.
Hypertension is common but non-specific; a home-cuff value is patient-reportable but a calibrated clinician measurement is sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Full blood count / haematocrit — markedly elevated haemoglobin and haematocrit

investigation_details.expected_result: Raised haemoglobin and haematocrit (elevated red-cell mass), often with raised platelets
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The defining measurement of hyperviscosity is missed; venesection cannot be targeted.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Haematocrit quantifies red-cell mass — the anchor of the diagnosis and of acute venesection. Not obtainable via telehealth.

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The myeloproliferative neoplasm is not molecularly confirmed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: JAK2 V617F confirms the myeloproliferative neoplasm — a laboratory molecular study, never available via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Aquagenic pruritus + erythromelalgia + plethora/injected conjunctivae + splenomegaly is a highly characteristic myeloproliferative pattern — but confirmation still requires bloods and JAK2.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Hyperviscosity syndrome with thrombotic risk (stroke / retinal vein occlusion)
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Worst-ever headache with transient visual disturbance and vertigo is the time-critical emergency driving escalation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Defining measurement of red-cell mass; guides emergency venesection. Hospital/pathology — not a telehealth capability.
Exclude ischaemic and haemorrhagic stroke in a worst-ever headache with neurological features before any antithrombotic is considered.
Confirms the myeloproliferative neoplasm; arranged through haematology, not remotely.
Low EPO supports primary polycythaemia over secondary causes.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: AI-issued remote 'thick blood' self-diagnosis or home venesection
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The AI must neither confirm the haematological diagnosis nor direct any venesection/bloodletting remotely — these are in-person/hospital actions.
